_about: Project-local rule pack consumed by src/auditdialog.cpp (loadUserRules).
User-defined rules run AFTER populateChecks() so the hardcoded catalogue
stays the baseline; entries here add new checks or override by id.
Origin: the 2026-04-22 four-dimensional review (perf / security / bugs /
refactor) produced findings that are expressible as repo-wide grep
patterns. Encoding them here turns the review into a persistent
regression guard instead of a one-off sweep.
Schema reference: see AuditDialog::loadUserRules in auditdialog.cpp.
Fields: id, name, description, category, command (required), type,

rules.name: QFontMetrics Construction (hoist-candidate)
rules.description: QFontMetrics construction. If inside a per-cell / per-frame loop, hoist it — QFontMetrics is non-trivial to build (font-shaping data) and the per-cell cost compounds across a viewport.

rules.name: QRegularExpression Not Marked Static
rules.description: QRegularExpression construction without the `static` qualifier on the declaration line. Static keeps the compiled pattern alive for subsequent calls; non-static re-compiles on every invocation. Flag for review — some construction sites (one-shot parse) legitimately don't need static.

rules.name: Vector insert/erase At begin()+N (rotate-candidate)
rules.description: `.erase(container.begin() + idx)` or `.insert(container.begin() + idx, …)` is O(n) per call. When the operation is inside a `for` loop over `count`, the loop is O(n·count). `std::rotate` does the same transformation in one linear pass. See ROADMAP 0.7.7+ §Perf — scrollUp/scrollDown rewrite.

rules.name: QString::toStdString in Hot Path
rules.description: Allocates a `std::string` copy of the QString's UTF-8 bytes. In the VT parse / render / PTY hot paths, every conversion compounds. Prefer QByteArray where possible, or pass QString across the API boundary.
rules.command: grep -nE '\.toStdString\(\)' src/terminalgrid.cpp src/terminalwidget.cpp src/vtparser.cpp src/ptyhandler.cpp src/glrenderer.cpp 2>/dev/null || true

rules.name: DBGLOG in VT Parse / Render Hot Path
rules.description: DBGLOG expands to a variadic macro that formats a string even when the log channel is disabled (fast path short-circuits at the first argument evaluation, but the call-site argument work — QString concatenation, .arg() — still runs). In the VT hot path this adds cost per byte parsed. Route through a channel check or drop the log.

rules.name: SSH Argv Construction Without -- Separator
rules.description: OpenSSH parses a host argument beginning with `-` as an option (CVE-2017-1000117 class — `-oProxyCommand=...` → RCE). Shell-quoting alone does not defend: the shell passes the argv correctly, ssh then re-interprets the leading dash. Insert a literal `--` before the host in any argv array that passes ssh a user-controlled host string.

rules.name: QImage::loadFromData Without QImageReader Size Peek
rules.description: Calling QImage::loadFromData on untrusted data commits to a full decode before any dimension check runs. A compression-bomb PNG (tiny compressed payload, huge declared dimensions) can force a multi-GB allocation. Use QImageReader::size() on a QBuffer wrapping the same data FIRST; reject if declared size exceeds MAX_IMAGE_DIM; only then decode.

rules.name: Shell-Quote Logic Duplicated Outside sshdialog.cpp
rules.description: `shellQuote` is implemented in sshdialog.cpp and re-implemented as a lambda in mainwindow.cpp. Two sources of truth drift apart. Consolidate into src/shellutils.h so a fix to one automatically benefits the other.

rules.name: Line Insert/Delete Without m_screenHyperlinks Sync
rules.description: `m_screenLines.insert/erase` inside terminalgrid.cpp must be paired with the same operation on `m_screenHyperlinks` — otherwise OSC 8 spans drift away from the cells containing the clickable text. Fixed in 0.7.7 for insertLines/deleteLines; guard rails to catch new copies of the pattern.
